code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an artist (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence. Handles a variety of predicates and creates complex
    sentences with relative clauses to connect related facts.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build predicate-to-phrase mapping
    def convert_predicate(subject: str, predicate: str, obj: str) -> str:
        """Convert a single triple to a natural language phrase."""
        mapping = {
            "birthDate": f"{subject} was born on {obj}",
            "deathDate": f"{subject} died on {obj}",
            "birthPlace": f"{subject} was born in {obj}",
            "deathPlace": f"{subject} died in {obj}",
            "origin": f"{subject} originates from {obj}",
            "nationality": f"{subject} is {obj}",
            "genre": f"{subject} performs {obj}",
            "instrument": f"{subject} plays the {obj}",
            "occupation": f"{subject} works as a {obj}",
            "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": f"{subject} is associated with {obj}",
            "recordLabel": f"{subject} is signed to {obj}",
            "activeYearsStartYear": f"{subject} started their career in {obj}",
            "activeYearsEndYear": f"{subject} ended their career in {obj}",
            "capital": f"The capital of {subject} is {obj}",
            "country": f"{subject} is located in {obj}",
            "language": f"The language of {subject} is {obj}",
            "officialLanguage": f"The official language of {subject} is {obj}",
            "parentCompany": f"{subject} is owned by {obj}",
            "bandMember": f"{subject} includes member {obj}",
            "leader": f"The leader of {subject} is {obj}",
            "leaderTitle": f"The leader title of {subject} is {obj}",
            "background": f"{subject} is a {obj}",
            "alternativeName": f"{subject} is also known as {obj}",
            "birthYear": f"{subject} was born in {obj}",
            "professionalField": f"{subject} works in {obj}",
            "musicFusionGenre": f"{subject} fuses {obj}",
            "musicSubgenre": f"{subject} is a subgenre of {obj}",
            "stylisticOrigin": f"{subject} originates from {obj}",
            "derivative": f"{subject} influenced {obj}",
            "location": f"{subject} is located in {obj}",
            "governingBody": f"{subject} is governed by {obj}",
            "ethnicGroup": f"{subject} has {obj} ethnic group",
            "distributingCompany": f"{subject} is distributed by {obj}",
            "training": f"{subject} trained at {obj}",
            "populationDensity": f"{subject} has a population density of {obj}",
            "meaning": f"{subject} means {obj}",
            "longName": f"{subject}'s long name is {obj}",
            "areaTotal": f"{subject} has an area of {obj}",
            "foundingDate": f"{subject} was founded in {obj}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"{subject} is {obj} above sea level",
            "postalCode": f"{subject} has postal codes {obj}",
            "isPartOf": f"{subject} is part of {obj}",
            "demonym": f"{subject} residents are called {obj}",
            "currency": f"{subject} uses {obj}",
            "anthem": f"{subject}'s anthem is {obj}",
        }
        return mapping.get(predicate, f"{subject} {predicate} {obj}")

    phrases = [convert_predicate(t.subject, t.predicate, t.object) for t in triples]

    if not phrases:
        return ""

    # Join phrases into a coherent sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        return phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        return ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples about an artist (or related entities) into a single,
      fluent English sentence. Handles a variety of predicates and joins multiple
      facts with commas and a final conjunction.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      phrases = []
      for t in triples:
          s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
  
          # Simple natural‑language rendering for common predicates
          if p == "birthDate":
              phrase = f"{s} was born on {o}"
          elif p == "deathDate":
              phrase = f"{s} died on {o}"
          elif p == "birthPlace":
              phrase = f"{s} was born in {o}"

              phrase = f"{s} died in {o}"
          elif p == "origin":
              phrase = f"{s} originates from {o}"
          elif p == "nationality":
              phrase = f"{s} is {o}"

              phrase = f"{s} performs {o}"
          elif p == "instrument":
              phrase = f"{s} plays the {o}"
